blocks:
Surah Al-Hijr

Meccan, and it is 99 verses.

Surah Al-Hijr

Meccan, and it is said (to be so) except for His saying: وَلَقَدْ آتَيْناكَ سَبْعاً مِنَ الْمَثانِي وَالْقُرْآنَ الْعَظِيمَ (15:87) (Certainly, We have given you the seven oft-repeated verses and the great Quran) and His saying: كَما أَنْزَلْنا عَلَى الْمُقْتَسِمِينَ الَّذِينَ جَعَلُوا الْقُرْآنَ عِضِينَ (15:90-91) (just as We sent down on the partitioners, those who made the Quran into parts). And it is ninety-nine verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) الر تِلْكَ آياتُ الْكِتابِ وَقُرْآنٍ مُبِينٍ. (1) Alif, Lām, Rā. These are the signs of the Book and a manifest Quran.

(٢) رُبَما And it was recited with takhfif (تخفيف) (lightening the bāʾ). يَوَدُّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَوْ كانُوا مُسْلِمِينَ (2) Many times will the faithless wish they had been Muslims. When they witness their state and the state of the Muslims, they will say: "Oh, would that we had been Muslims."

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him).

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the Day of Resurrection comes, a caller will call out from Allah: "No one shall enter Paradise except a Muslim." On that Day, يَوَدُّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لَوْ كانُوا مُسْلِمِينَ. (2) Many times will the faithless wish they had been Muslims.

And in al-Majma' (there is) what is similar in meaning.

And in it (al-Majma'), narrated marfu' (مرفوعاً) from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: When the inhabitants of the Fire gather in the Fire, and with them are those whom Allah willed from the People of the Qiblah (أهل القبلة), the disbelievers will say to the Muslims: "Were you not Muslims?" They will say: "Yes." They will say: "Then what benefit did your Islam bring you, seeing that you have become with us in the Fire?" They will say: "We had sins, and we were seized for them." Then Allah, exalted is His Name, will hear what they said, and He will command those who were in the Fire from the People of Islam to be brought out from it. It is then that the disbelievers will say: "Oh, would that we had been Muslims!" And another hadith concerning this has preceded in Surah Al-Baqarah regarding the Almighty's saying: لا يُقْبَلُ مِنْها شَفاعَةٌ وَلا يُؤْخَذُ مِنْها عَدْلٌ وَلا هُمْ يُنْصَرُونَ. (2:48) no intercession shall be accepted from it, nor shall any ransom be taken from it, nor will they be helped.

(٣) ذَرْهُمْ يَأْكُلُوا وَيَتَمَتَّعُوا وَيُلْهِهِمُ الْأَمَلُ فَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ (3) Leave them to eat and enjoy, and let hope distract them; soon they will know.

ذَرْهُمْ ** (Leave them) to eat and enjoy their worldly life وَيُلْهِهِمُ الْأَمَلُ ** (and let hope distract them)—and let their expectation of long lives (طول الأعمال) and stable conditions (استقامة الأحوال) distract them from preparing for the return (للمعاد)—then soon they will know the evil of their doing when they witness the recompense (الجزاء). And this is a notification that admonition (الوعظ) will not benefit them, nor will advice (النّصح) be effective in them; and it is an exaggeration in warning (الإنذار), an imposition of the proof (الحجّة), and a caution against preferring enjoyment (التنعم) and prolonging hope (تطويل الأمل).

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "I only fear two things for you: following caprice (اتباع الهوى) and prolonged hope (طول الأمَل). As for following caprice, it turns away from the truth (الحقّ); and as for prolonged hope, it causes forgetfulness of the Hereafter (الآخرة)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): "No servant prolonged hope except that he acted poorly (أساء العَمل)." And he used to say: "If the servant saw his term (أَجَله) and its swiftness toward him, he would despise working in pursuit of the world."

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), who said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "If the guardianship (ولاية) of Allah and happiness (السعادة) are deserved, the term (الأجل) comes between the eyes and hope (الأمل) goes behind the back. And if the guardianship (ولاية) of Satan (الشيطان) and wretchedness (الشقاوة) are deserved, hope (الأمل) comes between the eyes and the term (الأجَل) goes behind the back."

(٤) وَما أَهْلَكْنا مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ إِلَّا وَلَها كِتابٌ مَعْلُومٌ ** (4) We did not destroy any town but it had a known destiny. A predetermined term (أَجَل مقدر) written in the Preserved Tablet (اللّوحِ المحفوظ). (٥) ما تَسْبِقُ مِنْ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلَها وَما يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ ** (5) No nation can advance its term, nor can they postpone it. from it (the term). (٦) وَقالُوا يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِي نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ الذِّكْرُ ** (6) They say, 'O you, to whom the Reminder has been sent down, They called him by way of mockery (التهكّم) and ridicule (الاستهزاء), as indicated by what follows it: إِنَّكَ لَمَجْنُونٌ ** you are indeed crazy! because you speak the speech of the insane when you claim that Allah sent down the Reminder (الذّكر) upon you, meaning the Quran (القرآن).

(٧) لَوْ ما تَأْتِينا ** Why do you not bring us (هَلّا تَأْتِينَا) بِالْمَلائِكَةِ ** so that they may confirm you and support you in the call (الدعوة), like His saying: لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ فَيَكُونَ مَعَهُ نَذِيراً إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ ٧ (7) And they say, 'Why has not an angel been sent down to him, to be a warner along with him?' in your claim (دعواكَ).

(٨) ما نُنَزِّلُ الْمَلائِكَةَ (8) We do not send down the angels meaning (تَتَنَزَّلُ). And it was read with the tāʾ having a ḍammah (تُنَزِّلُ or تَتَنَزَّلُ), and with the nūn (نُنَزِّلُ), and with al-malāʾikah in the accusative case. إِلَّا بِالْحَقِ with wisdom (الحكمة) and benefit (المصلحة). وَما كانُوا إِذاً مُنْظَرِينَ meaning granted respite (ممهلين), that is, He does not grant them respite for an hour.

ما نُنَزِّلُ الْمَلائِكَةَ إِلَّا بِالْحَقِّ وَما كانُوا إِذاً مُنْظَرِينَ (We do not send down the angels except with the truth, and then they would not be granted any respite.)

Al-Qummi said: If We had sent down the angels, they would not have been granted respite, and they would have perished.

(٩) إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَزَّلْنَا الذِّكْرَ ** (Indeed, We have sent down the Reminder) This is a refutation of their denial and mockery, and for this reason, He confirmed it in several ways. وَإِنَّا لَهُ لَحافِظُونَ (and We will indeed guard it) from distortion (التحريف), alteration (التغيير), addition (الزيادة), and omission (النقصان).

(١٠) وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ فِي شِيَعِ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (Certainly, We have sent [apostles] before you among the factions of the former peoples) [Meaning] among their groups (فرقهم) and their parties (طوائفهم). And الشّيعة (al-Shi‘ah) is the group (الفرقة) when they agree upon a doctrine (مذهب) and a way (طريقة), derived from شاعه (shā‘ahu) when he followed him.

(١١) وَما يَأْتِيهِمْ مِنْ رَسُولٍ ** (and there did not come to them any apostle) [This is] a narration of a past state. إِلَّا كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ ** (but they used to ridicule Him) just as these people are doing. And this is a consolation for the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

(١٢) كَذلِكَ نَسْلُكُهُ ** (Thus do We insert it) It was said: We cause the Reminder (الذّكر) to enter and organize it [in their hearts]. فِي قُلُوبِ الْمُجْرِمِينَ ** (into the hearts of the guilty ones) meaning: We cast it into their hearts, denied and unacceptable. And it was said: The pronoun (الضمير) refers to the mockery (الاستهزاء).

(١٣) لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ ** (They do not believe in it) [Meaning] in the Reminder (الذكر). وَقَدْ خَلَتْ سُنَّةُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (and the precedent of the former peoples has already passed) meaning: the way (سُنّة) of Allah concerning them, by abandoning them and inserting disbelief (الكفر) into their hearts, or by destroying them when they denied their messengers. Thus, it is a threat (وعيداً) to the people of Mecca.

(١٤) وَلَوْ فَتَحْنا عَلَيْهِمْ ** (Even if We opened for them) [Meaning] upon these proposers [of signs]. باباً مِنَ السَّماءِ فَظَلُّوا فِيهِ يَعْرُجُونَ ** (a door in the sky, and they kept ascending through it) [meaning] they ascend to it throughout their day.

(١٥) لَقالُوا إِنَّما سُكِّرَتْ أَبْصارُنا ** (they would say, 'Our eyesight has only been blurred) [Meaning] our eyes (الأبصار) have been blocked by magic (السِّحر), and it has been imagined for us, without reality. And it was read سكرت (sukirat) with takhfif (التخفيف) [lightened kāf]. بَلْ نَحْنُ قَوْمٌ مَسْحُورُونَ ** (Rather, we are a bewitched people') [meaning] Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم has bewitched us with that.

(16) وَلَقَدْ جَعَلْنا فِي السَّماءِ بُرُوجاً (16) And certainly We have placed constellations in the heaven In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They are twelve constellations (بروج).

And in al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The constellations (البروج) are the stars (الكواكب). The constellations (البروج) belonging to spring and summer are Aries (الحمل), Taurus (الثور), Gemini (الجوزاء), Cancer (السّرطان), Leo (الأسد), and Virgo (السّنبلة). And the constellations (بروج) belonging to autumn and winter are Libra (الميزان), Scorpio (العقرب), Sagittarius (القوس), Capricorn (الجدي), Aquarius (الدّلو), and Pisces (الحوت). They are twelve constellations (بروج).

And in al-Qummi: They are the mansions (منازل) of the sun and the moon.

I say: The meaning of al-Buruj (البروج) is high palaces (القصور العالية). The stars (الكواكب) were named by this term because they are like dwellings (المنازل) for their inhabitants, the moving celestial bodies (للسّيّارات). Its derivation is from al-tabarruj (التبرّج) due to its appearance/manifestation (لظهوره).

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The sun has three hundred and sixty constellations (برجاً). Every constellation (برج) among them is like an island (جزيرة) among the islands (جزاير) of the Arabs. It descends into one of them every day. When it sets, it reaches the boundary of the interior of the Throne (بطنان العرش) and remains prostrating until the next day. Then it returns to the place of its rising, accompanied by two angels who call out with it.

I say: That is because the movement of the sun occurs in each of the twelve constellations (البروج الإِثني عشرَ) for approximately thirty days. Based on this consideration (الإِعتبار), each of them is divided into thirty constellations (برجاً), thus becoming three hundred and sixty.

وَزَيَّنَّاها لِلنَّاظِرِينَ (16) and We have adorned them for the observers In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): With the luminous stars (بالكواكب النيّرة).

(١٧) وَحَفِظْناها مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْطانٍ رَجِيمٍ ** (17) and We have guarded it from every outcast devil. So he is unable to ascend to it, or whisper (يوسوس) to its inhabitants, or interfere in its affairs, or become acquainted with its conditions. (١٨) إِلَّا مَنِ اسْتَرَقَ السَّمْعَ ** (18) except him who steals a hearing, who snatches it secretly فَأَتْبَعَهُ ** and overtakes him. شِهابٌ مُبِينٌ ** (a manifest flame) is apparent to those who observe. And the shihāb (الشهاب) is a bright flame of fire, and it may also be applied to the stars (الكواكب) and the spearhead (السِّنان) due to the brilliance (البريق) found in them both. In al-Majalis, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Iblis used to penetrate the seven heavens. When Jesus (peace be upon him) was born, he was veiled from three heavens, and he used to penetrate four heavens. When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was born, he was veiled from all seven of them, and the devils (الشياطين) were pelted with the stars. Quraysh said: "This is the establishment of the Hour (قيام الساعة) which we used to hear the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) mention." And 'Amr ibn Umayyah, who was one of the most eloquent poets (أرجز) of the Jāhiliyyah (الجاهلية), said: "Look at these stars by which guidance is sought and by which the seasons of winter and summer are known. If they are the ones being pelted, then it is the destruction of everything. But if they remain fixed and others are being pelted, then it is a matter that has occurred." The narrative (الحديث). And al-Qummi said: The devils (الشياطين) did not cease ascending to the heaven and spying until the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was born. Then he mentioned the saying of 'Amr ibn Umayyah and attributed it to Walīd ibn al-Mughīrah. Then he said: There was a Jew in Mecca named Yūsuf. When he saw the stars moving and traveling in the sky, he went out to the assembly (نادي) (1) of Quraysh and said: "O assembly of Quraysh, has a newborn been delivered among you tonight?" They said: "No." He said: "You are mistaken, by the Torah (التوراة), the last and best of the Prophets has been born this night. He is the one whom we find in our books that when that Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم is born, the devils (الشياطين) will be pelted and veiled from the heaven." So, every one returned to his dwelling and asked his family, and they said: "A child has been born to 'Abd Allāh ibn 'Abd al-Muṭṭalib ibn 'Abd Manāf." The narrative (الحديث).

footnote (١) The Arabian Peninsula (*جزيرة العرب*) is what is surrounded by the Indian Ocean (*بحر الهند*) and the Sea of Sham (*بحر الشّام*), then the Tigris (*دجلة*) and the Euphrates (*الفرات*), and what lies between 'Adan Abyan (*عدن أبين*) to the borders of Sham (*الشّام*) lengthwise, and from Jeddah (*جدّة*) to the countryside of Iraq (*ريف العراق*) breadthwise. M. N.

(19) وَالْأَرْضَ مَدَدْناها ** (19) And the earth We have spread out We spread it out (بَسَطْنَاهَا). وَأَلْقَيْنا فِيها رَواسِيَ ** and cast in it firm mountains firm mountains (جِبَالًا ثَوَابِتَ). وَأَنْبَتْنا فِيها مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مَوْزُونٍ ** and We have grown in it every kind of balanced thing. apportioned (مُقَدَّر).

Al-Qummi (said): For every type (ضَرْب) of animal, We have apportioned (قَدَّرْنَا) a measured thing (شَيْئًا مَوْزُونًا). And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, grew in the mountains gold (الذهب), silver (الفضّة), jewels (الجوهر), brass (الصّفر), copper (النّحاس), iron (الحديد), lead (الرّصاص), antimony (الكحل), arsenic (الزّرنيخ), and similar things, which are not sold except by weight (وزناً).

(٢٠) وَجَعَلْنا لَكُمْ فِيها مَعايِشَ ** (20) And We have provided livelihoods for you therein whereby you live, consisting of foods (المطاعم) and clothing (الملابس). وَمَنْ لَسْتُمْ لَهُ بِرازِقِينَ وَجَعَلْنا لَكُمْ مَنْ لَسْتُمْ لَهُ بِرازِقِينَ ** and for those whom you do not provide for, and We have provided for you those whom you do not provide for. consisting of dependents (العيال), servants (الخدم), slaves (المماليك), animals (الحيوانات), and all the rest of what you imagine that you provide for them—a false assumption (حسباناً كاذباً)—for indeed Allah provides sustenance for you and for them.

(٢١) **وَإِنْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا عِنْدَنا خَزائِنُهُ وَما نُنَزِّلُهُ إِلَّا بِقَدَرٍ مَعْلُومٍ ** (21) And there is nothing but that its treasuries are with Us, and We do not send it down except in a known measure.

It was said that the treasuries (الخزائن) are an expression for the power (القدرة) to bring it into existence (إيجاده).

Al-Qummi said: The treasury (الخزانة) is the water that descends from the sky, causing to grow for every kind of creature the sustenance (الغذاء) that Allah has decreed for it.

I say: The first is the speech of one who is devoid of realization (التحصيل), and the second is a metaphor (تمثيل) for the purpose of bringing understanding closer to the comprehension of the general populace (الجمهُور), and it is an interpretation (تفسير) of the apparent meaning (الظاهر). As for the inner meaning (الباطن) and the esoteric interpretation (التأويل), the treasuries (الخزائن) are an expression for what the Supreme Pen (القلم الأعلى) first wrote upon the universal aspect (الوَجه الكلّي) in the Tablet of Decree (لوح القضاء), which is preserved from alteration (التبديل). From this, it flows secondly upon the particular aspect (الوَجه الجزئي) in the Tablet of Destiny (لوح القدر), which contains effacement (المحو) and affirmation (الإثبات), gradually proceeding according to the sending down (التنزيل). The first is alluded to by His saying: وَإِنْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا عِنْدَنا خَزائِنُهُ ** (And there is nothing but that its treasuries are with Us), and by His saying: وَعِنْدَهُ أُمُّ الْكِتابِ (and with Him is the Mother of the Book). And the second is alluded to by His saying: وَما نُنَزِّلُهُ إِلَّا بِقَدَرٍ مَعْلُومٍ ** (and We do not send it down except in a known measure), and from this, it descends and manifests in the world of witnessing (عالم الشهادة).

And narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): That upon the Throne (العرش) is the likeness (تمثال) of everything Allah created, from the land (البر) and the sea (البحر). He said: "And this is the esoteric interpretation (تأويل) of His saying: وَإِنْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ ** (And there is nothing) [to the end of] the Ayah." He intended by this what We have mentioned. The complete verification (تحقيق) of this station (المقام) should be sought in Our book named ‘Ilm al-Yaqin (علم اليقين), for it is sufficient in its explanation.

footnote (١) والنّادي والنّدوة والمنتدى مجلس القوم نهاراً والمجلس ما داموا مجتمعين فيه وما يندوهم النّادي ما يسعهم ق. (1) Al-Nādī (النّادي), al-Nadwah (النّدوة), and al-Muntadā (المنتدى) refer to the gathering place (مجلس) of the people during the day, and the gathering (المجلس) is [the place] as long as they are assembled therein. And mā yandūhum al-nādī (ما يندوهم النّادي) means what accommodates them (ما يسعهم). (Q.)

footnote (٢) واتى بلفظة من دون لفظة ما لأنّه غلب العقلاء على غيرهم م ن (2) And He brought the word man (من) instead of the word mā (ما) because He gave precedence to the rational beings (العقلاء) over others. (M.N.)

(٢٢) وَأَرْسَلْنَا الرِّياحَ لَواقِحَ (And We send the fertilizing winds) القمّيّ said: (They are) those which fertilize the trees. And العيّاشيّ narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (أمير المؤمنين) عليه السلام, who said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Do not curse the wind, for it is good tidings (بُشر), and it is a warning (نذر), and it is fertilizing (لواقح). So ask Allah for its good and seek refuge in Him from its evil." فَأَنْزَلْنَا مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً فَأَسْقَيْنَاكُمُوهُ وَمَا أَنْتُمْ لَهُ بِخَازِنِينَ (٢٢) (Then We send down water from the sky, and provide it for you to drink, and you are not its keepers) He negated from them what He affirmed for Himself in His saying: وَإِنْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا عِنْدَنَا خَزَائِنُهُ (٢١) (There is not a thing but its treasuries are with Us) Meaning: We are the keepers (الخازنون) of the water, the Capable ones (القادرون) of creating it in the sky and sending it down from it, and you are not capable of that.

(٢٣) وَإِنَّا لَنَحْنُ نُحْيِي وَنُمِيتُ وَنَحْنُ الْوارِثُونَ. (And indeed it is We who give life and bring death, and We are the inheritors.) Al-Qummi (said): meaning, We inherit the earth and those upon it.

footnote (١) يعني ملاقح جمع ملقحة اي تلقح الشّجرة والسّحاب كأنّها تهيّجه ويقال لواقح جمع لاقح اي حوامل لأنّها تحمل السّحاب م. (1) It means مَلاقِح is the plural of مُلْقِحَة, meaning it fertilizes (تُلْقِحُ) the tree and the cloud, as if it stirs it up (تُهَيِّجُهُ). And it is said لَوَاقِح is the plural of لَاقِح, meaning bearing (حَوَامِل), because they carry the cloud (السَّحَاب).

(٢٤) وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْنَا الْمُسْتَقْدِمِينَ مِنْكُمْ وَلَقَدْ عَلِمْنَا الْمُسْتَأْخِرِينَ. (24) We certainly know those of you who go ahead, and We certainly know those who lag behind. Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): [They are] the believers (المؤمنون) from this community (الأمّة). (٢٥) وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ إِنَّهُ حَكِيمٌ عَلِيمٌ. (25) Indeed your Lord will gather them. Indeed He is all-wise, all-knowing. (٢٦) وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنْسانَ مِنْ صَلْصالٍ. (26) Certainly We created man out of dry clay. Al-Qummi said: It is the water (الماء) that rattles (المتصَلْصِل) with the clay (الطين). مِنْ حَمَإٍ مَسْنُونٍ ** (out of mud molded). He said: It means altered mud (حَمَأ متغيرّ). And in the narrative (حديث) concerning the creation of Adam: He (may His glory be exalted) scooped up a handful (غرفة) of water, caused it to rattle (فصَلْصَلها), and it solidified (فجمدت)—the narrative has already passed in Surat al-Baqarah. Al-Ṣalṣāl (الصّلصال) is said to be the dry clay (الطين اليابس) that rattles (يصلصل)—it makes a sound when struck—and it is unbaked (غير مطبوخ). If it is baked, then it is pottery (فخّار). Al-Ḥamaʾ (الحمأ) is the black, altered mud (الطين الأسود المتغيّر). Al-Masnūn (المسنون) is said to mean the molded (المصوّر), the poured and emptied (المصبوب المفرّغ), and the putrid (المنتَن). It is as if He poured the mud (الحمأ) and molded from it a hollow statue (تمثال إنسان أجوف) of a human, which then dried until, when struck, it rattled (صلصل), then it was altered (غيّر) and made into a human.

And in Nahj al-Balagha: Then He (glory be to Him) gathered soil (تربة) from the rugged (حَزْن) and the soft (سهلها) parts of the earth, from its sweet (عذبها) and its saline (سبخها) parts. He moistened it (سنّها) with water until it became pure (خلصت), and He kneaded it (ولاطها) with moisture (البلّة) until it became sticky (لزبت). Then He made from it a form (صورة) possessing bends (أحناءٍ), joints (وصول), limbs (أعضاء), and segments (فصول). He solidified it (أجمدها) until it held together (استمسكت), and He hardened it (أصلدها) until it rattled (صلصلت) for a determined time (لوقت معدود) and a known term (أجل معلوم). Then He breathed into it of His Spirit (روحه), and it was represented as a human (إنساناً) possessing minds (أذهان) that he imagines with, and thought (فكر) that he employs, and organs (جوارح) that he utilizes, and tools (أدوات) that he manipulates, and knowledge (معرفة) by which he distinguishes between tastes (الأذواق), smells (المشام), colors (الألوان), and species (الأجناس). [He was] kneaded with the clay (طينة) of different colors (الألوان المختلفة), harmonious similarities (الأشباه المؤتلفة), conflicting opposites (الأضداد المتعادية), and disparate mixtures (الأخلاط المتباينة) of heat (الحرّ), cold (البرد), moisture (البلّة), dryness (الجمود), sorrow (المساءة), and joy (السّرور)—the narrative continues.

(27) وَالْجَانَ ** means the father of the Jānn (أَبَا الجانّ).

Al-Qummi said: [It means] the father of Iblīs. خَلَقْناهُ مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (We created him before) means before the creation of man. مِنْ نارِ السَّمُومِ ** (from the fire of the Samūm) means from the fire of intense heat (الشّديد الحِّر) that penetrates the pores (المسامّ).

In al-Khiṣāl, narrated from al-Ṣādiq (peace be upon him): The fathers (الآباء) are three: Adam, who begot believers; the Jānn (الجانّ), who begot believers and disbelievers; and Iblīs, who begot disbelievers. There is no reproduction (نتاج) among them; rather, he lays eggs and hatches them (يبيض ويفرخ), and his offspring are males (ذكور) with no females (إناث) among them.

Al-Qummi said: The Jinn (الجنّ) are the offspring of the Jānn (الجانّ). Among them are believers and disbelievers, Jews and Christians, and their religions vary. The devils (الشياطين) are the offspring of Iblīs, and there are no believers among them except one whose name is Hām ibn Haym ibn Lāqīs ibn Iblīs. He came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, who saw him as a huge, enormous body and a terrifying man. He [the Prophet] said to him: "Who are you?" He replied: "I am Hām ibn Haym ibn Lāqīs ibn Iblīs. On the day Qābīl killed Hābīl, I was a young boy of a few years, forbidding adherence [to righteousness] (الاعتصام) and commanding the spoiling of food (إفساد الطعام)." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "By my life, wretched is the youth who is hoped for (المؤمّل) and the elder who is commanded (المؤمّر)." Hām said: "Leave this, O Muḥammad, for my repentance (توبتي) has been accepted at the hands of Nūḥ (Noah). I was with him in the Ark (السّفينَةِ) and I reproached him for his prayer against his people. I was with Ibrāhīm (Abraham) (peace be upon him) when he was thrown into the fire, and Allah made it cool and safe (برداً وسلاماً). I was with Mūsā (Moses) when Allah drowned Pharaoh and saved the Children of Israel. I was with Hūd when he prayed against his people, and I reproached him. I was with Ṣāliḥ, and I reproached him for his prayer against his people. I have read the Books, and all of them give me glad tidings of you, and the Prophets convey greetings (السلام) to you and say that you are the best and most noble of the Prophets. So, teach me something of what Allah has revealed to you." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Teach him." Hām said: "O Muḥammad, we do not obey anyone except a Prophet or the successor (وصيّ) of a Prophet. Who is this?" He [the Prophet] said: "This is my brother, my successor (وصيّي), my minister (وزيري), and my heir (وارثي), ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib (peace be upon him)." Hām said: "Yes, we find his name in the Books as Ilyā (إِليّا)." So, the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) taught him. When the Night of al-Harīr (ليلة الهرير) occurred at Ṣiffīn, he came to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

** (٢٨) وَإِذْ قالَ رَبُّكَ ** And remember the time of His saying لِلْمَلائِكَةِ إِنِّي خالِقٌ بَشَراً مِنْ صَلْصالٍ مِنْ حَمَإٍ مَسْنُونٍ.** (28) (And when your Lord said to the angels, 'Indeed, I am going to create a human out of dry clay, from an altered mud.)

(٢٩) فَإِذا سَوَّيْتُهُ ** I perfected his creation وَنَفَخْتُ فِيهِ مِنْ رُوحِي ** until its effects flowed through the cavities of his limbs, so he became alive فَقَعُوا لَهُ ساجِدِينَ. (29) (So when I have proportioned him and breathed into him of My Spirit, then fall down before him in prostration.')

In al-Ilal, al-Qummi, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): This was a prelude from Allah concerning Adam before He created him, and an argument (احتجاج) from Him against them. The narration (الحديث) has already preceded, along with its beginning and end, in Surat al-Baqarah regarding the Almighty's saying: إِنِّي جاعِلٌ فِي الْأَرْضِ خَلِيفَةً. (Indeed, I am going to set a vicegerent on the earth.)

In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the Almighty's saying: **وَنَفَخْتُ فِيهِ مِنْ رُوحِي ** (and breathed into him of My Spirit). He said: "It is a spirit that Allah chose, selected, and created, and attributed it to Himself, and favored it over all other spirits. Then He breathed from it into Adam."

In al-Tawhid and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and he said: "Indeed, Allah created a creation (خلقاً) and created a spirit (روحاً), then He commanded an angel to breathe into it. It is not something that diminished anything from Allah; it is from His power (قدرته)."

In al-Tawhid and al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked how this breathing (النفخ) occurs. He said: "Indeed, the spirit (الروح) is moving like the wind (الريح). It was only named روح because its name is derived from الريح (wind). It was only expressed using the word الروح because the spirit is kindred (مجانس) to the wind. He only attributed it to Himself because He selected it over the rest of the spirits, just as He selected a house among houses and said, 'My House (بيتي),' and He said of one of the Messengers, 'My intimate friend (خليلي),' and similar examples. It is created (مخلوق), fashioned (مصنوع), originated (محدث), sustained (مربوب), and managed (مدّبر)."

I say: Since the spirit (الروح) first attaches itself to the subtle vapor (البخار اللطيف) emanating from the heart and pours the animalistic power (القوّة الحيوانية) upon it, and then flows carrying it through the cavities of the arteries (الشرائين) to the depths of the body, its attachment to the body was designated as 'breathing' (نفخاً). Thus, it is a representation (تمثيل) of that by which life (الحيوة) is attained. This is because the spirit is not from the world of sense and visibility (عالم الحس والشهادة); rather, it is from the world of the Dominion (الملكوت) and the Unseen (الغيب). The body is merely a shell, a covering, and a mold for it. Its life is only through the spirit, and this is the 'other creation' (الخلق الآخر) referred to by His saying, the Glorified: **ثُمَّ أَنْشَأْناهُ خَلْقاً آخَرَ ** (Then We developed him into another creation). Meaning: a creation that does not resemble this creation.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the spirit (الروح), and he said: "It is from His power (قدرته), from the Dominion (الملكوت)." What indicates this is the preceding narrations in Surat Āl ‘Imrān regarding His saying, the Glorified: وَلا تَحْسَبَنَّ الَّذِينَ قُتِلُوا فِي سَبِيلِ اللهِ أَمْواتاً بَلْ أَحْياءٌ. (Do not suppose that those who were slain in the way of Allah are dead; rather, they are living.)

In al-Basa'ir, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The example of the believer and his body is like a jewel in a box. If the jewel is taken out of it, the box is discarded and no attention is paid to it. He also said: "Indeed, the spirits (الأرواح) do not mix with the body nor penetrate it; rather, they are like coverings (كالكلل) for the body, encompassing it."

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The spirit (الروح) is not described by heaviness or lightness. It is a subtle body (جسم رقيق) that has been clothed in a dense mold (قالباً كثيفاً). Thus, it is like the wind (الريح) in a waterskin (الزق). When it is blown into it, the waterskin becomes full of it, yet its entry does not increase the weight of the waterskin, nor does its exit decrease it. Likewise is the spirit; it has neither heaviness nor weight. It was asked: "Does the spirit vanish after leaving its mold, or does it remain?" He said: "Rather, it remains until the Day the Trumpet (الصور) is blown. At that point, things become nullified and perish, so there is neither sense (حسّ) nor sensible thing (محسوس). Then things are restored as their Manager (مدبّرها) initiated them, and that is four hundred years during which creation is forgotten, and that is between the two blasts (النفختين)."

He (peace be upon him) also said: "Indeed, the spirit remains in its place: the spirit of the virtuous (المُحسن) is in light and spaciousness, and the spirit of the evil-doer (المسيء) is in confinement and darkness, and the body turns into dust." (The narration).

It is narrated that he said: "And by it (the spirit), the body is commanded and forbidden, rewarded and punished. It may separate from the body, and Allah, the Glorified, clothes it with another [body] as His wisdom (حكمته) dictates." His saying, "It may separate from the body, and Allah clothes it with another [body]," is explicit that it is separate from the body and independent, and that the intended meaning is not the vaporous spirit (الرّوح البخاري). As for applying the term 'body' (الجسم) to it, this is because the realm of the Dominion (نشأة الملكوت) is also corporeal (جسمانيّة) in terms of form, even if it is spiritual (روحانية) in terms of meaning, and is not perceived by these senses. As for his saying, "Thus, it is like the wind in a waterskin," this is a representation (تمثيل) of that by which life is attained, and a clarification of the meaning of its being breathed into the body, as was alluded to just now.

It should be known that the spirits (الأرواح) are multiple in the human body, and their number increases with the increase of the person's merit (الفضل) and honor (الشرف), as has been widely narrated (استفاض) in the reports from the Pure Imams (الأئمّة الأطهار). In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): A man came to him and said: "O Commander of the Faithful, some people claim that a servant does not commit fornication while he is a believer, nor steal while he is a believer, nor drink wine while he is a believer, nor consume usury (الرّبا) while he is a believer, nor shed unlawful blood while he is a believer. This has become heavy upon me, and my chest is constrained by it, since I claim that this servant prays my prayer, supplicates my supplication, and I marry him and he marries me, and I inherit from him and he inherits from me, yet he has departed from faith (الإِيمان) because of a minor sin he committed." The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "You have spoken truly (1). I heard the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم say—and the proof for this is the Book of Allah—that Allah created people in three classes (طبقات) and settled them in three stations (منازل) (1). That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in the Book: أَصْحابُ الْمَيْمَنَةِ وَأَصْحابُ الْمَشْئَمَةِ وَالسَّابِقُونَ ** (The People of the Right Hand, and the People of the Left Hand, and the Foremost). As for what he mentioned concerning the most powerful (أَمَرّ) (2) of the Foremost (السابقون), they are the Prophets, both sent and unsent. Allah placed five spirits in them: the Spirit of Holiness (روح القدس), the Spirit of Faith (روح الإِيمان), the Spirit of Strength (روح القوّة), the Spirit of Desire (روح الشّهوة), and the Spirit of the Body (روح البدن). By the Spirit of Holiness, they were sent as Prophets, both sent and unsent, and by it they knew things. By the Spirit of Faith, they worshipped Allah and did not associate anything with Him. By the Spirit of Strength, they fought their enemy and managed their livelihoods. By the Spirit of Desire, they attained delicious food and married lawful women [in the prime of youth] (شوابّ شباب النساءِ). By the Spirit of the Body, they moved and walked. These are those who are forgiven and whose sins are overlooked. Then he said: Allah Almighty said: تِلْكَ الرُّسُلُ فَضَّلْنا بَعْضَهُمْ عَلى بَعْضٍ مِنْهُمْ مَنْ كَلَّمَ اللهُ وَرَفَعَ بَعْضَهُمْ دَرَجاتٍ وَآتَيْنا عِيسَى ابْنَ مَرْيَمَ الْبَيِّناتِ وَأَيَّدْناهُ بِرُوحِ الْقُدُسِ ** (These apostles—We have preferred some of them over some others: among them are those to whom Allah spoke, and some of them He raised in rank, and We gave Jesus son of Mary manifest proofs and strengthened him with the Holy Spirit). Then He said concerning their group: وَأَيَّدَهُمْ بِرُوحٍ مِنْهُ (and strengthened them with a spirit from Him). He means He honored them with it, thus preferring them over others. These are those who are forgiven and whose sins are overlooked. Then he mentioned the People of the Right Hand (أصحاب الميمنة), who are the true believers (المؤمنون حقّاً) themselves. Allah placed four spirits in them: the Spirit of Faith, the Spirit of Strength, the Spirit of Desire, and the Spirit of the Body. The servant continues to perfect these four spirits until conditions come upon him." The man asked: "O Commander of the Faithful, what are these conditions?" He said: "As for the first of them, it is as Allah said: وَمِنْكُمْ مَنْ يُرَدُّ إِلى أَرْذَلِ الْعُمُرِ لِكَيْ لا يَعْلَمَ بَعْدَ عِلْمٍ شَيْئاً ** (and among you is he who is relegated to the most abject age, so that he knows nothing after [having had] some knowledge). In this state, all the spirits diminish in him, but he is not one who departs from the religion of Allah, because the One Who caused this is He Who relegated him to the most abject age. Thus, he does not know the time for prayer, nor is he able to perform night or day vigil (التهجّد), nor stand in the row with the people. This is a deficiency in the Spirit of Faith, but it does not harm him at all. Among them is he whose Spirit of Strength diminishes, so he is unable to fight his enemy or seek livelihood. Among them is he whose Spirit of Desire diminishes, so if the most beautiful daughters of Adam passed by him, he would not yearn for them nor rise [in arousal]. The Spirit of the Body remains in him, so he moves and walks until the Angel of Death comes to him. This person is in a state of goodness, because Allah is the Doer of this to him. And conditions may come upon him in his strength and youth, where he intends a sin, and the Spirit of Strength encourages him, and the Spirit of Desire beautifies it for him, and the Spirit of the Body leads him until it causes him to fall into the sin. When he touches it, he suffers a deficiency in Faith (الإِيمان) and it separates from him, and it does not return to him until he repents. If he repents, Allah accepts his repentance, but if he returns [to the sin], Allah will admit him to the Fire of Hell. As for the People of the Left Hand (أصحاب المشأمة), they are the Jews and the Christians. Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: الَّذِينَ آتَيْناهُمُ الْكِتابَ يَعْرِفُونَهُ كَما يَعْرِفُونَ أَبْناءَهُمْ (Those whom We have given the Book recognize him just as they recognize their own children). They recognize Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Wilayah (الولاية) in the Torah and the Gospel just as they recognize their children in their homes. وَإِنَّ فَرِيقاً مِنْهُمْ لَيَكْتُمُونَ الْحَقَّ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ الْحَقُّ مِنْ رَبِّكَ ** (Indeed, a part of them conceal the truth while they know it. The truth is from your Lord)—that you are the Messenger to them—فَلا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُمْتَرِينَ ** (So do not be among the doubters). When they denied what they knew, He afflicted them with that, stripping them of the Spirit of Faith and settling three spirits in their bodies: the Spirit of Strength, the Spirit of Desire, and the Spirit of the Body. Then He likened them to cattle, saying: إِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا كَالْأَنْعامِ ** (They are only like cattle), because the beast (الدّابّة) is only carried by the Spirit of Strength, feeds by the Spirit of Desire, and moves by the Spirit of the Body." The questioner said: "You have revived my heart by the permission of Allah, O Commander of the Faithful."

It is narrated from Kumayl ibn Ziyad that he said: I asked our Master, the Commander of the Faithful, Ali (peace be upon him), and said: "O Commander of the Faithful, I want you to make me know my soul (نفسي)." He said: "O Kumayl, which of the souls (الأنفس) do you want me to make you know?" I said: "O my Master, is it not just one soul?" He said: "O Kumayl, they are four: the Vegetative Growing Soul (النّامية النباتية), the Animalistic Sensory Soul (الحِسِّيّة الحيوانية), the Holy Rational Soul (الناطقة القدسيّة), and the Divine Universal Soul (الكليّة الإِلهيّة). Each of these has five powers (قوى) and two characteristics (خاصيتان). The Vegetative Growing Soul has five powers: retaining (ماسكة), attracting (جاذبة), digesting (هاضمة), expelling (دافعة), and nourishing (مربيّة). It has two characteristics: increase (الزيادة) and decrease (النقصان), and its source (انبعاثها) is the liver (الكبد). The Animalistic Sensory Soul has five powers: hearing (سمع), sight (بصر), smell (شمّ), taste (ذوق), and touch (لمس). It has two characteristics: contentment (الرضا) and anger (الغضب), and its source is the heart (القلب). The Holy Rational Soul has five powers: thought (فكر), memory (ذكر), knowledge (عِلمٌ), forbearance (حِلم), and alertness (نباهة). It has no source [in the body], and it is the most similar thing to the angelic souls (النفوس الملكيّة). It has two characteristics: purity (النزاهة) and wisdom (الحكمة). The Divine Universal Soul has five powers: permanence (بقا) in annihilation (فناء), bliss (نعيم) in hardship (شقاء), honor (عزّ) in humiliation (ذلّ), poverty (فقر) in wealth (غناء), and patience (صبر) in affliction (بلاءٍ). It has two characteristics: contentment (الرضا) and submission (التسليم). This is the one whose origin is from Allah and to Him it returns. Allah said: وَنَفَخْتُ فِيهِ مِنْ رُوحِي (and breathed into him of My Spirit). And the Almighty said: يا أَيَّتُهَا النَّفْسُ الْمُطْمَئِنَّةُ ارْجِعِي إِلى رَبِّكِ راضِيَةً مَرْضِيَّةً ** (O tranquil soul! Return to your Lord, well-pleased and pleasing [to Him]). And the intellect (العقل) is the center of all."

(1) A clarification: صدقت (You have spoken truly) is based on the passive construction (البناء للمفعول), meaning: they spoke truly concerning what they claimed, and he is not one who departs from the religion of Allah. If it is said that it is established that a person is resurrected upon the state in which he died, so if the elderly person dies without knowledge (معرفة), how will he be resurrected knowing? I say: Since what prevented him from attending to his knowledge (معارفه) was an accidental matter (أمراً عارضاً), when that was removed by death, his knowledge, which was latent in his essence (ذاته), became manifest to him. This is unlike one who never attained knowledge at all, for there is nothing in his essence to become manifest to him. (Wafi).

(1) Three stations (ثلاث منازل) refers to three ranks (مراتب) mentioned for the three types of spirits (الأرواح الثّلاثة). The summary of the answer is that the perpetrator of a major sin (الكبيرة) without persistence (الإصرار) is not included among the People of the Left Hand (أصحاب المشأمة), because those mentioned in their rank are those who يُصِرُّونَ عَلَى الْحِنْثِ الْعَظِيمِ (persist in the great sin). Thus, they are included among the People of the Right Hand (أصحاب الميمنة).

(2) أَمَرّ (with fathah on the mīm and tashdīd on the unpointed letter) means: the strongest (أقوى) and most intelligent (أعقل), derived from المرّة (with kasrah), which means strength (القوّة) and intensity of intellect (شدّة العقل).

(٣٠) فَسَجَدَ الْمَلائِكَةُ كُلُّهُمْ أَجْمَعُونَ. (30) So the angels prostrated, all of them together, (٣١) إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ أَبى أَنْ يَكُونَ مَعَ السَّاجِدِينَ. (31) except Iblis, who refused to be with those who prostrated. (٣٢) قالَ يا إِبْلِيسُ ما لَكَ أَلَّا تَكُونَ مَعَ السَّاجِدِينَ. (32) He said, 'O Iblis! What is your excuse that you are not with those who prostrated?' (٣٣) قالَ لَمْ أَكُنْ لِأَسْجُدَ ** (33) He said, 'I am not one to prostrate It is not proper for me, and it contradicts my state, being a spiritual angel (مَلَك رُوحَانِيّ), that I should prostrate to a dense physical human (بَشَرٍ جِسْمَانِيّ كَثِيف) خَلَقْتَهُ مِنْ صَلْصالٍ مِنْ حَمَإٍ مَسْنُونٍ ** whom You created from a dry clay, from an altered mud.' and it is the basest of the elements. وخَلَقْتَنِي مِنْ نارٍ ** and You created me from fire,' and it is the noblest of them. Arrogance (الحميّة) deceived him, wretchedness (الشقوة) overcame him, and he felt superior due to the creation from fire and deemed the creation from clay (الصلصال) weak. His answer has already preceded in Surat al-A'raf along with other words.

(٣٤) قالَ فَاخْرُجْ مِنْها ** (34) He said, 'Begone from it! From the station (المنزلة) that you hold in the heaven (السّماءِ) and among the company (زمرة) of the angels. فَإِنَّكَ رَجِيمٌ (for you are an outcast) meaning: expelled (مطرود) from goodness (الخير) and honor (الكرامة). A narration concerning the meaning of al-rajīm (الرّجيم) has previously occurred in the discussion of seeking refuge (الاستعاذة). (٣٥) وَإِنَّ عَلَيْكَ اللَّعْنَةَ إِلى يَوْمِ الدِّينِ ** (35) and the curse shall be on you till the Day of Retribution.' For that is the limit (منتهى أمد) of the duration of the curse (اللّعن). (٣٦) قالَ رَبِّ فَأَنْظِرْنِي ** (36) He said, 'My Lord! Respite me Meaning: grant me a delay (فأَمهلني) إِلى يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ ** (until the day they are resurrected.') He desired to find an opportunity (فسحَة) for misguidance (الإغواءِ) and salvation (نجاة) from death (الموت). A narration concerning its reason (سببه) has previously occurred in Sūrat al-A‘rāf. (٣٧) قالَ فَإِنَّكَ مِنَ الْمُنْظَرِينَ (37) He said, 'You are indeed among the reprieved (٣٨) إِلى يَوْمِ الْوَقْتِ الْمَعْلُومِ. (38) until the day of the known time.' In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and He said: يَوْمِ الْوَقْتِ الْمَعْلُومِ ** (the day of the known time) is the day when the Trumpet (الصور) is blown a single blast (نفخة واحدة), and Iblīs dies between the first and the second blast (النفخة الأولى والثانية). And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and He said: "Do you suppose that it is the day when people are resurrected? Rather, Allah reprieved him until the day when Our Qā’im (قائمنا) is raised. When Allah raises Our Qā’im, He will be in the Mosque of Kūfah (مسجد الكوفة), and Iblīs will come until he kneels (يجثو) before Him on his knees, and he will say: 'Woe to him (يا ويله) from this day!' Then He will seize him by his forelock (ناصيته) and strike his neck (عنقه). That is يَوْمِ الْوَقْتِ الْمَعْلُومِ. (the day of the known time)." And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), He said: يَوْمِ الْوَقْتِ الْمَعْلُومِ ** (the day of the known time) is the day when the Messenger of Allah (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) slaughters him (يذبحه) upon the Rock (الصخرة) that is in Bayt al-Maqdis (بيتِ المقدس). I say: This means during the Return (الرّجعة).

(٣٩) قالَ رَبِّ بِما أَغْوَيْتَنِي ** (39) He said, 'My Lord! Because You have led me astray, Because of Your leading me astray, which is His imposing upon him (Iblis) that which resulted in error (الغي). لَأُزَيِّنَنَّ لَهُمْ ** I will surely make [things] fair-seeming for them on the earth, acts of disobedience (المعاصِيَ) فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَأُغْوِيَنَّهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ and I will surely lead them all astray, (٤٠) إِلَّا عِبادَكَ مِنْهُمُ الْمُخْلَصِينَ ** (40) except Your exclusive servants among them.' Those whom You have made sincere for Your obedience and purified them from impurities (الشوائب), so my cunning (كيدي) will not affect them. And it was recited with a kasrah on the lām (الْمُخْلِصِينَ), meaning: those who made their souls sincere for You. (٤١) قالَ هذا صِراطٌ عَلَيَ ** (41) He said, 'This is a straight path [leading] to Me. Meaning: this is a path of truth which is incumbent upon Me to observe. مُسْتَقِيمٌ ** there is no deviation from it, and that is that you shall have no authority over My sincere servants. And it was recited عليّ (ʿalīy) with the pattern of فَعِيل (faʿīl) and in the nominative case (بالرّفع*). And he attributed it in al-Majma' to al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and it is interpreted as high honor (علوّ الشرف*). And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): هذا صِراط عَلَي مُسْتَقِيمٌ (This is a straight path belonging to ʿAlī). And this also admits the possibility of annexation (الإضافة). And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): It is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

(٤٢) إِنَّ عِبادِي لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطانٌ إِلَّا مَنِ اتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ الْغاوِينَ (١) (Indeed, as for My servants, you have no authority over them, except the misguided ones who follow you.) An explanation of what was stated generally. Al-Ayyashi narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: That He was asked about its interpretation, and He said: Allah said: "Indeed, you do not possess the power to admit them into Paradise or the Fire."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "By Allah, He did not intend by this except the Imams and their followers (شيعتهم)."

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Him عليه السلام: That which is similar in meaning.

(٤٣) وَإِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ لَمَوْعِدُهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ ** (43) Indeed, hell is the tryst of them all. [It is] the tryst of the misguided followers (المتّبعين) (٢). Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): [It refers to] their standing upon the Bridge (الصراط).

footnote (١) Because if he accepts from him, he gains authority (*سلطان*) over him by his deviation from guidance to that which he calls him to, namely, the following of caprice (*الهوى*). And it was said that the exception (*الاستثناء*) is discontinuous (*منقطع*), and the meaning is: but whoever followed you among the misguided (*الغاوين*) granted you authority (*سلطاناً*) over himself.

footnote (٢) Meaning, the tryst of Iblis and those who followed him.

(٤٤) لَها سَبْعَةُ أَبْوابٍ لِكُلِّ بابٍ مِنْهُمْ جُزْءٌ مَقْسُومٌ. (44) It has seven gates, and for every gate there is a designated portion of them.

Al-Qummi said: The people of a specific religious community (أهل ملّة) will enter through every gate.

In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his father, from his grandfather (peace be upon them): That the Fire has seven gates: a gate through which Pharaoh (فرعون), Haman (هامان), and Qarun (قارون) will enter; and a gate through which the polytheists (المشركون), the disbelievers (الكفّار), and those who did not believe in Allah for the blink of an eye will enter; and a gate through which Banu Umayyah (بنو أمّية) will enter, which is exclusively for them; no one will crowd them in it. And it is the gate of Ladhā (لظى), and it is the gate of Sa'īr (سعير), and it is the gate of al-Hāwiyah (الهاوية). They will fall through it for seventy autumns (خريفاً). And whenever He causes them to fall for seventy autumns, He will cause them to boil with a surge, and He will cast them up to its highest part for seventy autumns. Then He will cause them to fall like this for seventy autumns, and they will remain perpetually thus, abiding eternally, forever. And a gate through which those who hate us, those who fight us, and those who abandon us will enter, and it is the greatest of the gates and the most intense in heat. Then he said: And the gate through which Banu Umayyah enter is specifically for Abu Sufyan (أبي سفيان), Mu'awiyah (معاوية), and the House of Marwan (آل مروان). They will enter through that gate, and the Fire will crush them therein with a crushing (حَطماً); no cry will be heard from them, and they will neither live therein nor die.

And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The seven gates of the Fire are matched (مطابقات).

In al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): That Hell (جهنّم) has seven gates, layers (أطباق) one above the other. And he placed one of his hands upon the other and said: "Like this." And indeed, Allah placed the Gardens (الجنان) horizontally (على العرض) and placed the Fires (النّيران) one above the other. So, the lowest of them is Jahannam (جهنّم), and above it is Ladhā (لظى), and above it is al-Hutamah (الحطمة), and above it is Saqar (سقر), and above it is al-Jahīm (الجحيم), and above it is al-Sa'īr (السعير), and above it is al-Hāwiyah (الهاوية). He said: And in one narration: The lowest of them is al-Hāwiyah (الهاوية) and the highest of them is Jahannam (جهنّم).

And Al-Qummi mentioned seven degrees (درجات), then he mentioned their detailed explanation extensively in another manner, but he did not mention their inhabitants (أصحابها).

(٤٥) إِنَّ الْمُتَّقِينَ فِي جَنَّاتٍ وَعُيُونٍ. (45) The Godwary will be amid gardens and springs. (٤٦) ادْخُلُوها بِسَلامٍ آمِنِينَ ** (46) ‘Enter them in peace and safety.’ (This is) based on the intention of the saying (i.e., the command is implied to be said to them). (٤٧) وَنَزَعْنا ما فِي صُدُورِهِمْ مِنْ غِلٍ. And the Almighty's saying: (47) We will remove whatever rancour there is in their breasts. Al-Qummi (said it means) enmity (العداوة). إِخْواناً عَلى سُرُرٍ مُتَقابِلِينَ. as brothers, facing one another on couches. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "By Allah, you are those about whom Allah said: وَنَزَعْنا ما فِي صُدُورِهِمْ** (We will remove whatever rancour there is in their breasts), [the rest of] the Ayah." And in a narration: "By Allah, He did not intend anyone other than you by this."

(٤٨) لا يَمَسُّهُمْ فِيها نَصَبٌ ** (No fatigue shall touch them in it) fatigue and hardship (تعب وعناء) وَما هُمْ مِنْها بِمُخْرَجِينَ ** (nor shall they be expelled from it) for the perfection of bliss is through eternity (الخلود). (٤٩) نَبِّئْ عِبادِي أَنِّي أَنَا الْغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ. (Inform My servants that I am indeed the All-forgiving, the All-merciful) (٥٠) وَأَنَّ عَذابِي هُوَ الْعَذابُ الْأَلِيمُ ** (and that My punishment is the painful punishment.) So hope for My mercy and fear My punishment. (٥١) وَنَبِّئْهُمْ عَنْ ضَيْفِ إِبْراهِيمَ. (And inform them about the guests of Abraham.) (٥٢) إِذْ دَخَلُوا عَلَيْهِ فَقالُوا سَلاماً ** (When they entered upon him and said, 'Peace!') We greet you with a greeting (سلاماً) قالَ إِنَّا مِنْكُمْ وَجِلُونَ ** (He said, 'We are apprehensive of you.') fearful (خائفون), and that was because they refrained from eating, as preceded in Surat Hud. (٥٣) قالُوا لا تَوْجَلْ إِنَّا نُبَشِّرُكَ بِغُلامٍ عَلِيمٍ. (They said, 'Do not be apprehensive. We give you the good news of a knowledgeable boy.') Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The knowledgeable boy (الغلام العليم) is Isma'il (اسمعيل), from Hajar (هاجر). And from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): So Ibrahim (ابراهيم) remained for three years after the good news, then the good news came to him from Allah concerning Isma'il (اسمعيل), repeatedly, after three years.

(٥٤) قالَ أَبَشَّرْتُمُونِي عَلى أَنْ مَسَّنِيَ الْكِبَرُ ** (54) He said, 'Do you bring me good news when old age has already overtaken me?' He expressed astonishment that a child would be born to him despite old age having overtaken him, so [he asked] "with what do you bring me good news?" (فَبِمَ تُبَشِّرُونَ) for this is something whose occurrence is not normally conceivable. (٥٥) قالُوا بَشَّرْناكَ بِالْحَقِ ** (55) They said, 'We bring you good news in truth.' [Meaning] with that which will certainly come to pass, with certainty. فَلا تَكُنْ مِنَ الْقانِطِينَ ** 'So do not be one of the despairing ones.' [Meaning] from those who despair of that, for He, the Exalted, is capable of it, since just as He acts through manifest means (الأسباب الجلّية), He also acts through hidden means (الأسباب الخفيّة). (٥٦) قالَ وَمَنْ يَقْنَطُ مِنْ رَحْمَةِ رَبِّهِ إِلَّا الضَّالُّونَ ** (56) He said, 'And who despairs of his Lord's mercy except the astray ones?' Meaning, those who err in the path of knowledge (المعرفة), thus they do not know the vastness of Allah's mercy and the perfection of His power. And it was recited يَقْنِط with a kasrah on the nūn. (٥٧) قالَ فَما خَطْبُكُمْ ** (57) He said, 'What is your business, [Meaning] after the good news. أَيُّهَا الْمُرْسَلُونَ. O messengers?' (٥٨) قالُوا إِنَّا أُرْسِلْنا إِلى قَوْمٍ مُجْرِمِينَ ** (58) They said, 'We have been sent to a guilty people,' Meaning the people of Lūṭ, for they were a wicked people, so that We may warn them of the punishment of the Lord of all the worlds. This is according to al-ʿIlal and al-ʿAyyāshī, narrated from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him). And in al-ʿIlal, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: Lūṭ and Ibrāhīm (peace be upon them both) continuously awaited the descent of the punishment upon the people of Lūṭ. Ibrāhīm (peace be upon him) and Lūṭ held a noble status with Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. And whenever Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, intended to punish the people of Lūṭ, the affection (مودّة) of Ibrāhīm (peace be upon him), his close friendship (خلّته), and the love of Lūṭ would reach Him, so He would observe them and postpone their punishment. He said: When Allah's wrath (أسف) intensified upon the people of Lūṭ, and He decreed their punishment, and decided to compensate Ibrāhīm for the punishment of the people of Lūṭ with a knowing boy (غلام عليم)—thereby consoling him for his affliction due to the destruction of the people of Lūṭ—Allah sent messengers to Ibrāhīm (peace be upon him) to bring him the good news of Ismāʿīl. They entered upon him at night, and he was startled by them and feared they might be thieves. When the messengers saw him startled and terrified, they said: "Peace (سَلاماً)." He said: "Peace (سَلامٌ). We are apprehensive of you (إِنَّا مِنْكُمْ وَجِلُونَ)." They said: "Do not fear (لا تَوْجَلْ). We are the messengers of your Lord. نُبَشِّرُكَ بِغُلامٍ عَلِيمٍ ** (We bring you the good news of a knowing boy)." He said: And the knowing boy (الغلام العليم) is Ismāʿīl, from Hājar. So Ibrāhīm (peace be upon him) said to the messengers: أَبَشَّرْتُمُونِي عَلى أَنْ مَسَّنِيَ الْكِبَرُ ** (Do you bring me good news when old age has already overtaken me?)—[and so on to the end of] the āyāt. And al-ʿAyyāshī, narrated from him (peace be upon him), said: When Allah, the Exalted, decreed the punishment of the people of Lūṭ and determined it, He loved to compensate Ibrāhīm (peace be upon him) for the punishment of the people of Lūṭ with a knowing boy (غلام عليم), thereby consoling him for his affliction due to the destruction of the people of Lūṭ—the ḥadīth is as mentioned [above].

(٥٩) إِلَّا آلَ لُوطٍ إِنَّا لَمُنَجُّوهُمْ (59) except the family of Lot. We will indeed deliver them And it was read with takhfif (التخفيف) أَجْمَعِينَ. (all of them.) (٦٠) إِلَّا امْرَأَتَهُ قَدَّرْنا ** (60) except his wife. We determined And it was read with takhfif (التخفيف) إِنَّها لَمِنَ الْغابِرِينَ ** (that she will be among those who remain behind)—those who remain (الباقين) with the disbelievers, so that she may perish with them.

Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Woe to the Qadariyyah (القدريّة)! They only recite this verse: إِلَّا امْرَأَتَهُ قَدَّرْنا إِنَّها لَمِنَ الْغابِرِينَ (except his wife. We determined that she will be among those who remain behind). Woe to them! Who determined (قدّرها) her [fate] except Allah, the Blessed and Exalted?

(٦١) فَلَمَّا جاءَ آلَ لُوطٍ الْمُرْسَلُونَ. (61) When the envoys came to Lot’s family, (٦٢) قالَ إِنَّكُمْ قَوْمٌ مُنْكَرُونَ ** (62) he said, ‘You are an unfamiliar folk.’ My soul finds you unfamiliar (تُنْكِرُكُمْ نَفْسِي) and shies away from you, fearing that you might approach me with evil (بِشَرّ). (٦٣) قالُوا بَلْ جِئْناكَ بِما كانُوا فِيهِ يَمْتَرُونَ ** (63) They said, ‘No, we have brought you what they used to doubt, namely, the punishment of Allah. (٦٤) وَأَتَيْناكَ بِالْحَقِ ** (64) and we have brought you the truth, so that you may warn your people of the punishment وَإِنَّا لَصادِقُونَ. and We are indeed truthful. (٦٥) فَأَسْرِ ** (65) So set out Travel secretly/by night بِأَهْلِكَ ** with your family O Lot بِقِطْعٍ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ ** in a watch of the night, when half the night has passed وَاتَّبِعْ أَدْبارَهُمْ ** and follow behind them, and be upon their trail, so that you may be a watchful eye (عَيْناً) over them, and none of them lags behind وَلا يَلْتَفِتْ مِنْكُمْ أَحَدٌ ** and none of you shall look back, to what is behind him **وَامْضُوا حَيْثُ تُؤْمَرُونَ ** and go where you are commanded.’ to where you have been commanded to go.

(٦٦) وَقَضَيْنا إِلَيْهِ ** (And We decreed to him) (Meaning: to Lot) ذلِكَ الْأَمْرَ ** (that matter/decree) (Meaning: from them; what follows explains it) أَنَّ دابِرَ هؤُلاءِ ** (that the last remnant of these) (Meaning: their last) مَقْطُوعٌ ** (will be cut off) (Meaning: they will be uprooted completely, until none of them remains) **مُصْبِحِينَ ** (by morning) (Meaning: entering into the morning.)

وَقَضَيْنا إِلَيْهِ ذلِكَ الْأَمْرَ أَنَّ دابِرَ هؤُلاءِ مَقْطُوعٌ مُصْبِحِينَ (66) And We decreed to him that matter: that the last remnant of those [people] would be cut off by morning.

(٦٧) وَجاءَ أَهْلُ الْمَدِينَةِ The city of Sodom يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ rejoicing at Lot's guests, desiring them. (67) And the people of the city came, rejoicing, (٦٨) قالَ إِنَّ هؤُلاءِ ضَيْفِي فَلا تَفْضَحُونِ by disgracing my guests, for whoever harms his guest has harmed him. (68) He said, 'These are my guests, so do not disgrace me, (٦٩) وَاتَّقُوا اللهَ regarding committing the abomination (الفاحشة) وَلا تُخْزُونِ And do not humiliate me, derived from الخزي meaning disgrace (الهوان), or do not shame me, derived from الخزاية meaning modesty (الحياء). (69) and be wary of Allah, and do not humiliate me.' (٧٠) قالُوا أَوَلَمْ نَنْهَكَ عَنِ الْعالَمِينَ It has previously been narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that what is meant by this is the prohibition against hosting and lodging people. (70) They said, 'Did we not forbid you from [entertaining] the people of the world?' (٧١) قالَ هؤُلاءِ بَناتِي إِنْ كُنْتُمْ فاعِلِينَ Its interpretation has preceded. (71) He said, 'Here are my daughters, if you are going to do [anything].' (٧٢) لَعَمْرُكَ. Al-Qummi [said]: meaning, "By your life, O Muhammad." He said: This is a distinction (فضيلة) for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم over the prophets. إِنَّهُمْ لَفِي سَكْرَتِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ They are in their misguidance (غوايتهم) which has removed their intellects, wandering aimlessly. So how can they listen to advice (النّصح)? (72) By your life! they are bewildered in their stupor.

(٧٣) فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الصَّيْحَةُ ** (73) Then the Cry seized them the cry of Gabriel (جبرئيل) مُشْرِقِينَ ** while they were rising. entering the time of sunrise. (٧٤) فَجَعَلْنا عالِيَها ** (74) Thus We made its uppermost part the uppermost part of their town سافِلَها ** its nethermost, and it became overturned with them. وَأَمْطَرْنا عَلَيْهِمْ حِجارَةً مِنْ سِجِّيلٍ ** and rained upon them stones of Sijjīl. made of hardened clay. (٧٥) إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِلْمُتَوَسِّمِينَ ** (75) There are indeed signs in that for the percipient, the discerning (المتفرّسين) ones who fix their gaze until they know the reality of the thing by its mark (بسمته). (٧٦) وَإِنَّها ** (76) and it is indeed It was said: and its traces لَبِسَبِيلٍ مُقِيمٍ ** on an enduring way. a fixed way (ثابت) that people traverse, and it has not been obliterated yet, and they see those traces. This is an admonition to Quraysh, like His saying: وَإِنَّكُمْ لَتَمُرُّونَ عَلَيْهِمْ مُصْبِحِينَ. (And you pass by them in the morning.)

In al-Majma', it is authentically narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that He said: "Beware of the discernment (فراسة) of the believer, for he looks with the light of Allah." And He said: "Indeed, Allah has servants who know people through perception (بالتوسّم)," then He recited this Ayah.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, he said that the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام said regarding the Almighty's saying: إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآياتٍ لِلْمُتَوَسِّمِينَ (There are indeed signs in that for the percipient): "The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was the percipient (المتوسّم), and I am after him, and the Imams from my progeny are the percipient ones (المتوسّمون)."

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him عليه السلام regarding this Ayah, he said: "They are the Imams." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Beware of the discernment (فراسة) of the believer, for he looks with the light of Allah," concerning this Ayah.

And from al-Sadiq عليه السلام, it is narrated that He was asked about this Ayah, and He said: "We are the percipient ones (المتوسّمون), and the way (السبيل) is enduring in us."

And al-Qummi added: "And the way (السبيل) is the path to Paradise." And narrated from him عليه السلام regarding: وَإِنَّها لَبِسَبِيلٍ مُقِيمٍ (and it is indeed on an enduring way), He said: "It never departs from us."

In al-Basa'ir, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: "There is no creature except that between his eyes is written 'believer' or 'disbeliever,' and that is veiled from you, but it is not veiled from the Imams عليهم السلام from the Household of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them). Then, no one enters upon them except that they know him, whether he is a believer or a disbeliever." Then He recited this Ayah.

In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "When the Riser (القائم) عليه السلام rises, no one from the creation of the All-beneficent stands before Him except that He knows whether he is righteous (صالح) or wicked (طالح). And in Him is a sign for the percipient ones (للمتوسّمين), and He is the enduring way (السبيل المقيم)." And al-Ayyashi narrated from him عليه السلام: "In the Imam عليه السلام is a sign for the percipient ones (للمتوسّمين), and He is the enduring way (السبيل المقيم). He looks with the light of Allah and speaks on behalf of Allah; nothing of what He wills is hidden from Him."

(٧٧) إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (77) Indeed, there is a sign in that for the faithful. (٧٨) وَإِنْ كانَ وانّه كان أَصْحابُ الْأَيْكَةِ يعني الغَيضَة وهي الشجَرة المتكاثفة لَظالِمِينَ هم قوم شعيب كانوا يسكنون الغيضة فبعثه الله إليهم فكذّبوه فأهلكوا بالظّلّة. (78) وَإِنْ كانَ أَصْحابُ الْأَيْكَةِ لَظالِمِينَ (78) And the inhabitants of Aykah were indeed wrongdoers. وَإِنْ كانَ ** (And though they were) and they were أَصْحابُ الْأَيْكَةِ ** (the inhabitants of Aykah) meaning the thicket (الغَيضَة), which is the dense, intertwined trees (الشجَرة المتكاثفة) لَظالِمِينَ ** (indeed wrongdoers). They are the people of Shu'ayb, who used to inhabit the thicket (الغيضة). So Allah sent Him to them, but they denied Him, and thus they were destroyed by the cloud-shadow (الظّلّة). (٧٩) فَانْتَقَمْنا مِنْهُمْ بالإهلاك وَإِنَّهُما يعني سدوم والأيكة لَبِإِمامٍ مُبِينٍ لبطريق واضح يؤمّ ويتّبع ويهتدي. (79) فَانْتَقَمْنا مِنْهُمْ وَإِنَّهُما لَبِإِمامٍ مُبِينٍ (79) So We took vengeance on them, and indeed both of them are on a manifest highway. فَانْتَقَمْنا مِنْهُمْ ** (So We took vengeance on them) by destruction (بالإهلاك) وَإِنَّهُما ** (and indeed both of them) meaning Sodom (سدوم) and Aykah (الأيكة) لَبِإِمامٍ مُبِينٍ ** (are on a manifest highway), meaning a clear path (لبطريق واضح) that is sought (يؤمّ), followed (ويتّبع), and guided by (ويهتدي). (٨٠) وَلَقَدْ كَذَّبَ أَصْحابُ الْحِجْرِ الْمُرْسَلِينَ يعني ثمود كذّبوا صالحاً والحجر واديِهم وهو ما بين المدينة والشام وكانوا يسكنونها. (80) وَلَقَدْ كَذَّبَ أَصْحابُ الْحِجْرِ الْمُرْسَلِينَ (80) Certainly the inhabitants of al-Ḥijr denied the apostles. وَلَقَدْ كَذَّبَ أَصْحابُ الْحِجْرِ الْمُرْسَلِينَ ** (Certainly the inhabitants of al-Ḥijr denied the apostles). Meaning Thamud (ثمود), they denied Salih (صالحاً). And al-Hijr (الحجر) is their valley (واديِهم), which is between Medina (المدينة) and Sham (الشام), and they used to inhabit it. (٨١) وَآتَيْناهُمْ آياتِنا كالناقة وسقيها وشربها ودرّها فَكانُوا عَنْها مُعْرِضِينَ. (81) وَآتَيْناهُمْ آياتِنا فَكانُوا عَنْها مُعْرِضِينَ. (81) We gave them Our signs, but they disregarded them. وَآتَيْناهُمْ آياتِنا ** (We gave them Our signs) such as the she-camel (الناقة), its watering (وسقيها), its drinking (وشربها), and its milk (ودرّها) فَكانُوا عَنْها مُعْرِضِينَ. (but they disregarded them). (٨٢) وَكانُوا يَنْحِتُونَ مِنَ الْجِبالِ بُيُوتاً آمِنِينَ من الانهدام ونقب اللصوص وتخريب الأعداءِ لوثاقتها أو من العذاب لفرط غفلتهم. (82) وَكانُوا يَنْحِتُونَ مِنَ الْجِبالِ بُيُوتاً آمِنِينَ (82) They used to hew out houses in the mountains, feeling secure. وَكانُوا يَنْحِتُونَ مِنَ الْجِبالِ بُيُوتاً آمِنِينَ ** (They used to hew out houses in the mountains, feeling secure) from collapse (من الانهدام), from the breaking in of thieves (ونقب اللصوص), and from the destruction by enemies (وتخريب الأعداءِ) due to their solidity (لوثاقتها), or secure from the punishment (من العذاب) due to their excessive heedlessness (لفرط غفلتهم). (٨٣) فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الصَّيْحَةُ مُصْبِحِينَ. (83) فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الصَّيْحَةُ مُصْبِحِينَ. (83) So the Cry seized them at sunrise. (٨٤) فَما أَغْنى عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ من بناء البيوت الوثيقة واستكثار الأموال والعدد. (84) فَما أَغْنى عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ (84) What they used to earn did not avail them. فَما أَغْنى عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ ** (What they used to earn did not avail them) such as building solid houses (من بناء البيوت الوثيقة) and accumulating wealth and numbers (واستكثار الأموال والعدد). (٨٥) وَما خَلَقْنَا السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَما بَيْنَهُما إِلَّا بِالْحَقِ فلا يلائم استمرار الفساد ودوام الشّرّ فلذلك اقتضت الحكمة إهلاك أمثال هؤلاءِ وإزاحة فسادهم من الأرض وَإِنَّ السَّاعَةَ لَآتِيَةٌ فينتقم الله لك فيها مِمّن كذّبك فَاصْفَحِ الصَّفْحَ الْجَمِيلَ. (85) وَما خَلَقْنَا السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَما بَيْنَهُما إِلَّا بِالْحَقِ وَإِنَّ السَّاعَةَ لَآتِيَةٌ فَاصْفَحِ الصَّفْحَ الْجَمِيلَ. (85) We did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between them except with the truth, and the Hour is surely coming. So forgive with a graceful forgiveness. وَما خَلَقْنَا السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَما بَيْنَهُما إِلَّا بِالْحَقِ ** (We did not create the heavens and the earth and what is between them except with the truth). Thus, the continuation of corruption (استمرار الفساد) and the permanence of evil (دوام الشّرّ) are incompatible [with this truth]. Therefore, wisdom necessitated the destruction of people like these and the removal of their corruption (فسادهم) from the earth. وَإِنَّ السَّاعَةَ لَآتِيَةٌ ** (and the Hour is surely coming). So Allah will take vengeance for you therein upon those who denied you. فَاصْفَحِ الصَّفْحَ الْجَمِيلَ (So forgive with a graceful forgiveness). في العيون عن الرضا عليه السلام : يعني العفو من غير عتاب. In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): It means pardon (العفو) without reproach (غير عتاب).

(٨٦) إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ الْخَلَّاقُ ** (86) Indeed your Lord is the All-creator, Who created you and created them, and in His hand is your affair and their affair, the All-Knowing (الْعَلِيمُ) of your state and their state. Thus, He is worthy that you entrust (the matter) to Him so that He may judge between you.

(٨٧) وَلَقَدْ آتَيْناكَ سَبْعاً مِنَ الْمَثانِي وَالْقُرْآنَ الْعَظِيمَ ** (87) Certainly We have given you the seven oft-repeated ones and the Great Quran. Al-Mathani (الْمَثَانِي) is derived from al-Tathniyah (التثنية) (repetition) or al-Thana' (الثناء) (praise).

In al-Uyun, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ ** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful) is an Ayah of the Opening of the Book (Fatihat al-Kitab), and it consists of seven Ayat, completed by بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ . I heard the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم say: "Indeed, Allah said to me: 'O Muhammad, وَلَقَدْ آتَيْناكَ سَبْعاً مِنَ الْمَثانِي وَالْقُرْآنَ الْعَظِيمَ ** (Certainly We have given you the seven oft-repeated ones and the Great Quran).' So He singled out the favor upon me with the Opening of the Book (Fatihat al-Kitab) and placed it opposite the Great Quran."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and he said: "It is Surat al-Hamd (The Chapter of Praise), and it consists of seven Ayat, including بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ . It was only named al-Mathani (الْمَثَانِي) because it is repeated (تُثَنَّى) in the two units of prayer (al-rak'atayn)." And narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them): He was asked about it, and he said: "It is the Opening of the Book (Fatihat al-Kitab), in which the saying is repeated (يُثْنَى)."

And likewise in al-Majalis, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him).

And in al-Majma', narrated from Ali, al-Baqir, and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them).

And in al-Qummi, (it is stated) that it is al-Fatihah. And in al-Kafi, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "I was given the Long Chapters (al-Suwar al-Tiwal) in place of the Torah, and I was given the Chapters of the Hundreds (al-Mi'in) in place of the Gospel (al-Injil), and I was given al-Mathani (الْمَثَانِي) in place of the Psalms (al-Zabur)."

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a long tradition: "Allah granted Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم additionally the Seven Long Chapters (al-Sab' al-Tiwal), and the Opening of the Book (Fatihat al-Kitab), which is the Seven Oft-Repeated Ones (al-Sab' al-Mathani), and the Great Quran."

And in al-Tawhid, al-Ayyashi, and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "We are al-Mathani (الْمَثَانِي) which Allah gave to our Prophet."

Al-Saduq (may his resting place be pleasant) said: His saying, "We are al-Mathani (الْمَثَانِي)," means: We are those whom the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم joined to the Quran, and he enjoined adherence to the Quran and to us, and informed his Ummah that we shall not separate until we reach his Pond (Hawd).

I say: Perhaps they (peace be upon them) were counted as seven based on their names, for they are seven. Based on this, it is permissible that al-Mathani (الْمَثَانِي) be derived from al-Thana' (الثناء) (praise), or that it be derived from al-Tathniyah (التثنية) (repetition) based on their being paired (تَثْنِيَتِهِمْ) with the Quran, or that it be made a metonymy (كِنَايَة) for their number, which is fourteen, by making himself (the Prophet) one of them through the conceptual distinction (التَّغَايُر الْاِعْتِبَارِيّ) between the giver and the recipient.

(٨٨) لا تَمُدَّنَّ عَيْنَيْكَ ** (Do not extend your eyes) Do not cast your gaze with the ambition of one who desires إِلى ما مَتَّعْنا بِهِ أَزْواجاً مِنْهُمْ ** (to what We have provided certain groups of them with) groups (أصنافاً) of the disbelievers, for indeed it is contemptible compared to what you have been given. وَلا تَحْزَنْ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (and do not grieve for them) if they do not believe, so that Islam and its people might be strengthened by them. وَاخْفِضْ جَناحَكَ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ** (and lower your wing to the faithful) and be humble towards those of the faithful who are with you, and be gentle with them, and be content regarding the faith of the wealthy and the powerful.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Whoever has been given the Quran, yet thinks that someone among the people has been given something better than what he has been given, has indeed magnified what Allah has belittled and belittled what Allah has magnified."

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): When this Ayah was revealed: لا تَمُدَّنَّ عَيْنَيْكَ ** (Do not extend your eyes), the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Whoever does not seek solace (يَتَعَزَّ) with the solace (عزاءِ) of Allah, his soul will be cut off by regrets over the world. And whoever casts his gaze upon what is in the hands of others, his worry will increase and his anger will not be cured. And whoever does not recognize that Allah has bestowed a favor upon him except in food or clothing, his action has fallen short and his punishment is near. And whoever wakes up grieving over the world, wakes up displeased with Allah. And whoever complains about a calamity that has befallen him, he is only complaining about his Lord. And whoever enters the Fire from this Ummah, among those who recited the Quran, he is one of those who took the signs of Allah in ridicule (هُزُواً). And whoever approaches a person of wealth (ذا ميسرة) and humbles himself before him seeking what is in his hands, two-thirds of his religion is gone."

And in al-Majma': The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would not look at what was considered desirable of the world.

(٨٩) **وَقُلْ إِنِّي أَنَا النَّذِيرُ الْمُبِينُ ** (89) And say: 'I am indeed the manifest warner.'

I warn you with clarity (ببيان) and proof (وبرهان) that the punishment of Allah will descend upon you if you do not believe, and I make clear to you what you need and that with which I was sent to you.

footnote (١) العزاء (*al-ʿazāʾ*) with *madd* is patience (*الصّبر*). It is said: *ʿazā yaʿzā* (from the pattern of *taʿiba*), meaning he was patient concerning what afflicted him. What is meant by seeking solace (*التعزّي*) through the solace of Allah (*بعزاء الله*) is enduring patience (*التصبّر*) and consolation (*التسلّي*) at the time of affliction. And its motto (*شِعَارُهُ*) is to say: إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ راجِعُونَ (Indeed we belong to Allah, and indeed to Him shall we return). And the meaning of *bi-ʿazāʾ Allāh* (*بعزاء الله*) is through Allah consoling him (*بتعزية الله إيّاه*). Thus, the noun (*الاسم*) is substituted for the verbal noun (*المصدر*). م.

(٩٠) كَما أَنْزَلْنا عَلَى الْمُقْتَسِمِينَ. (just as We sent down on the partitioners) (٩١) الَّذِينَ جَعَلُوا الْقُرْآنَ عِضِينَ ** (—those who broke the Qur’an into parts.) It was said: meaning, We sent down upon you the like of what We sent down upon the Jews and the Christians who made the Qur'an into portions (أَجزاء) and limbs (أعضاء). And they said, due to their obstinacy (عنادهم), that some of it is truth, conforming to the Torah and the Gospel, and some of it is falsehood, contradicting them. So they partitioned it into truth and falsehood. And it was said: (It means) the like of the punishment (العذاب) which We sent down upon them.

And al-Qummi said: They divided the Qur'an and did not compile it according to what Allah revealed. And al-Ayyashi narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): that they were asked about this Ayah, and they both said: "They are Quraysh." And narrated from one of them (peace be upon him) regarding الَّذِينَ ** (those who) who brought forth الْقُرْآنَ عِضِينَ ** (the Qur'an in parts): They said: "They are Quraysh."

(٩٢) فَوَ رَبِّكَ لَنَسْئَلَنَّهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ. (92) So by your Lord, We will surely question them all (٩٣) عَمَّا كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** (93) concerning what they used to do. and He will recompense them for it. (٩٤) فَاصْدَعْ بِما تُؤْمَرُ ** (94) So proclaim what you have been commanded, So declare it openly (فاجهر به) and manifest it (وأظهره). Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding the Almighty's saying: وَلا تَجْهَرْ بِصَلاتِكَ وَلا تُخافِتْ بِها ** (And do not be loud in your prayer, nor be low in it). He said: It was abrogated (نسختها) by [the Ayah]: فَاصْدَعْ بِما تُؤْمَرُ وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (So proclaim what you have been commanded, and disregard the polytheists). So do not pay attention to what they say.

(٩٥) إِنَّا كَفَيْناكَ الْمُسْتَهْزِئِينَ ** (95) Indeed We suffice you against the mockers, by crushing them and destroying them. (٩٦) الَّذِينَ يَجْعَلُونَ مَعَ اللهِ إِلهاً آخَرَ فَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ ** (96) those who set up another god besides Allah. Soon they will know. the consequence of their affair in the two abodes (الدّارين).

In al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم remained hidden, concealing himself and fearful, for five years, not revealing his mission. Ali (peace be upon him) and Khadijah were with him. Then Allah commanded Him to proclaim what He was commanded, so he appeared and revealed his mission. He said—and in another narration: three years.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him) that he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم remained hidden in Mecca for years, not revealing himself, while Ali (peace be upon him) and Khadijah were with him. Then Allah commanded Him to proclaim what He was commanded, so he appeared and began presenting himself to the tribes of the Arabs. When he came to them, they said: "A liar! Go away from us."

And al-Qummi (narrated): It was revealed in Mecca three years after the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was commissioned as a Prophet. He mentioned the hadith in a more detailed manner than what is in al-Ikmal. He said: The mockers of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم were five: Al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah, Al-As ibn Wa'il, Al-Aswad ibn al-Muttalib, Al-Aswad ibn Abd Yaghuth, and Al-Harith ibn Talatilah al-Khuza'i.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that he said: The mockers were five men from Quraysh. He mentioned these individuals, then said: When Allah said: **إِنَّا كَفَيْناكَ الْمُسْتَهْزِئِينَ ** (Indeed We suffice you against the mockers), the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم knew that He had disgraced them. So Allah caused them to die the worst of deaths.
