And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from his father, from his forefathers, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them all), in a hadith: As for the mockers, Allah said: إِنَّا كَفَيْناكَ الْمُسْتَهْزِئِينَ ** (Indeed We suffice you against the mockers). So Allah killed all five of them, each one by a death different from that of his companion, on a single day. As for Al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah, he passed by an arrow belonging to a man from Khuza'ah, which he had feathered (راشه) (1) and placed on the road. A splinter (شظيّة) (2) struck him, severing his median vein (اكحله) (3) until it bled him. He died saying: "The Lord of Muhammad has killed me."

As for Al-As ibn Wa'il al-Sahmi, he went out for a need of his to a certain place, and a rock rolled down beneath him, causing him to fall. He was torn piece by piece, and he died saying: "The Lord of Muhammad has killed me."

As for Al-Aswad ibn Abd Yaghuth, he went out to meet his son Zam'ah and sought shade under a tree. Jibril (جبرئيل) came to him, took his head, and struck it against the tree. He said to his servant: "Ward this off from me!" The servant replied: "I do not see anyone doing anything to you except yourself." So He killed him, and he died saying: "The Lord of Muhammad has killed me."

As for Al-Aswad ibn al-Muttalib, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم prayed against him that his sight be blinded and that he be bereaved of his children. When that day came, he went out until he reached a place. Jibril (جبرئيل) came to him with a green leaf and struck his face with it, so he became blind. He remained alive until Allah bereaved him of his children.

As for Al-Harith ibn al-Talatilah, he went out of his house during the scorching wind (السموم) and transformed into an Abyssinian (حبشيّاً). He returned to his family and said: "I am Al-Harith." They became angry with him and killed him, and he died saying: "The Lord of Muhammad has killed me."

He said: And it is narrated: That Al-Aswad ibn Abd Yaghuth ate a salted fish, and thirst afflicted him. He kept drinking water until his stomach burst, and he died saying: "The Lord of Muhammad has killed me." All of this happened in a single hour. This was because they were before the Messenger of Allah and said: "O Muhammad, we will wait for you until noon. If you retract your statement, otherwise we will kill you." The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم entered his house and closed the door upon himself, grieved by their words. Jibril (جبرئيل) came to him from Allah immediately and said: "O Muhammad, al-Salam (Peace) conveys al-Salam (greetings of peace) to you, and He says: فَاصْدَعْ بِما تُؤْمَرُ وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (94) So proclaim what you are commanded, and turn away from the polytheists." Meaning: Reveal your mission to the people of Mecca and invite them to faith. He said: "O Jibril (جبرئيل), what should I do about the mockers and what they have threatened me with?" And He (Allah) said to him: إِنَّا كَفَيْناكَ الْمُسْتَهْزِئِينَ ** (Indeed We suffice you against the mockers). He said: "O Jibril (جبرئيل), they were just now before me." He replied: "You have been sufficed against them." So, he revealed his mission at that point.

And al-Qummi, after mentioning the mockers and the manner in which sufficiency was granted against them, said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم went out and stood upon the Stone (الحجر). He said: "O assembly of Quraysh! O assembly of Arabs! I invite you to bear witness that there is no god except Allah and that I am the Messenger of Allah. I command you to cast off rivals (الأنداد) and idols (الأصنام). Respond to me, and by it you will rule the Arabs, the non-Arabs (العجم) will submit to you, and you will be kings in Paradise." They mocked him and said: "Muhammad ibn Abd Allah is mad (جنّ)." But they did not dare to harm him due to the position of Abu Talib.

(1) Rāsha al-sahm yarīshuhu means he affixed the feathers to the arrow, like karīshuhu (ق). (2) And al-shaziyyah (الشّظيّة) is the bow, the shin bone, and every splinter (فلقة) from a thing (Qamus). (3) And al-akhal (الأكحل) is a vein in the middle of the forearm, which is often bled (Nihayah). Or the vein of life (عرق الحياة) (ق).

(٩٧) وَلَقَدْ نَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ يَضِيقُ صَدْرُكَ بِما يَقُولُونَ ** (97) We certainly know that your breast is constrained by what they say. [This refers to] their denial of you, and their slandering of you and the Quran. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means regarding what He mentions concerning the virtue of His successor (وصيّه). (٩٨) فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَكُنْ مِنَ السَّاجِدِينَ ** (98) So celebrate the praise of your Lord, and be among those who prostrate. So seek refuge in Allah concerning what afflicts you, through glorification (التسبيح), praise (التحميد), and prayer (الصلوة). He will suffice you against anxiety (الهمّ) and remove distress (الغمّ) from you. In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "You must adhere to patience (الصّبر) in all your affairs, for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and commanded him to have patience (الصّبر) and gentleness (الرّفق). So he was patient until they afflicted him with great calamities (العظائم) and cast them upon him, and his breast became constrained. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: وَلَقَدْ نَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ يَضِيقُ صَدْرُكَ ** (We certainly know that your breast is constrained) [the rest of] the Ayah." And in al-Majma', [it is narrated that] the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, when an affair grieved him, would hasten to prayer (الصلوة).

**(٩٩) وَاعْبُدْ رَبَّكَ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَكَ الْيَقِينُ ** (99) And worship your Lord until certainty comes to you. And persist in the worship of your Lord until death comes to you, meaning, as long as you are alive. And the virtue of reading this Surah was mentioned previously at the end of Surah Ibrahim (peace be upon him).
